rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Proper Responses To Requests For Admission
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 24, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.¿s motion to compel responses to requests for admission is continued on the court¿s own motion to August 31 to allow the parties to continue meet and confer efforts. If any issues remain unresolved, the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:00 pm on August 30 specifying, without argument, those issues. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE EGGLETON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Varian Medical Systems, Inc. To Compel Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 24, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Varian Medical Systems, Inc.¿s motion to compel discovery responses is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN CASEY et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Its Requests For Admission, Compel Verifications To Plaintiffs¿ Responses To Special Interrogatories, Request For Admissions, Request For Production Of Documents And Form Interrogatories, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, August 24, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Carrier Corporation¿s motion to compel further responses to discovery and is granted. No opposition filed. Defendant is awarded $860 (4 hours at $215 per hour) in sanctions. = (220/HEK)
